examples.title: Process Creation with fork() and exec()
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a child process using fork() and executing a new program using execl()
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates fork() to create a child process and execl() (part of exec family) to replace the child's program with 'ls -la'. The parent uses wait() to synchronize with child termination.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for each system call, overall O(n) where n is execution time of child process
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for parent, child gets copy of parent address space O(n)

examples.title: File Operations using System Calls
examples.description: Shows file operations using Python which internally use system calls like open(), read(), write(), close()
examples.code:
import os

# Open a file (internally calls open() system call)
fd = os.open("example.txt", os.O_CREAT | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)

print(f"File descriptor: {fd}")

# Write to file (calls write() system call)
message = "Hello, this is a test message.\n"
os.write(fd, message.encode())

# Close the file (calls close() system call)
os.close(fd)

# Read the file back
fd = os.open("example.txt", os.O_RDONLY)
content = os.read(fd, 100)
os.close(fd)

print(f"Content read: {content.decode()}")

# Using high-level Python file operations
with open("example.txt", "r") as f:
    print(f"Using high-level IO: {f.read()}")

# Get file information (calls stat() system call)
stat_info = os.stat("example.txt")
print(f"File size: {stat_info.st_size} bytes")
print(f"File permissions: {oct(stat_info.st_mode)[-3:]}")
examples.explanation: Python's os module provides direct wrappers around system calls. os.open() maps to open(), os.read() to read(), os.write() to write(), os.close() to close(), and os.stat() to stat() system call.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the size of data being read/written
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for buffer used in read/write operations

examples.title: Inter-Process Communication using Pipes
examples.description: Demonstrates creating and using pipes for parent-child communication
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates pipe() system call to create a communication channel between parent and child. Parent writes to pipe using write(), child reads using read(). The pipe acts as a unidirectional buffer in kernel space.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the size of data transferred through the pipe
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the pipe buffer and message storage

examples.title: Getting Process and System Information
examples.description: Shows Information Maintenance system calls to get process and system information
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates various Information Maintenance system calls: getpid(), getppid(), getuid(), geteuid(), getgid(), getegid(), getcwd(), uname(), and time(). These allow programs to discover their execution environment.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for all information retrieval calls
